query: VHS scanlines TV static analog distortion CRT screen
message: sub:analog_degradation/VHS_CRT — anti_aesthetic — scanlines, TV static, color bars, pixel grids, retro CRT

response.objective_caption: A bulky CRT television sits in a dimly lit room, its screen filled with bright blue and white static. An articulated metal desk lamp arches over the top of the set, next to a black Sony electronics component and a Maxell tape case perched on the gray bezel. Color-coded yellow, white, and red RCA cables protrude from the bottom front of the television, while the surrounding shelves and walls fall into deep shadow.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A bulky CRT television sits in a dimly lit room, its screen overtaken by analog degradation in the form of bright blue static and a faint horizontal roll band that evokes aged analog media and tracking artifacts. An articulated metal desk lamp arches over the top of the set, next to a black Sony electronics component and a Maxell tape case perched on the gray bezel, with color-coded RCA cables visible at the bottom front and deep shadows swallowing the surrounding environment.
